positions.name: MeasuringInterStageDeadTime
positions.frame: How much of the extraction engine's wall clock is inter-stage dead time, and where does it concentrate across the pipeline?
positions.why: src-1 frames the finding as coming from instrumenting the multipass engine; src-2 quantifies the aggregate share of wall clock with no LLM call in flight; src-3 breaks that aggregate into the three specific inter-stage gaps (navigate-to-resolve, resolve-to-position, position-to-narrative). Read together they establish the empirical baseline the rest of the investigation (cache bug, fix, TTL) responds to — a diagnostic scoping move, not yet a cause or a remedy.

positions.name: SelfInvalidatingGraphCacheDiagnosis
positions.frame: What causes the corpus graph cache to invalidate itself against the very transaction the run just wrote?
positions.why: src-4 names the self-invalidating cache as the largest contributor to the dead time; src-5 specifies the cache key (owner, repo, ref, tree sha); src-6 identifies that both the consult prefetch and the navigator's post-agent reads were resolving that key against the run's own work branch; src-7 traces the causal chain to completion — the source-spur commit moves the tree sha mid-run, so the navigator's read misses the warm cache and rebuilds the full 64,000-quad corpus for one transaction. Read as a single causal account building toward the bug's mechanism, held prior to any fix.

positions.name: BaseRefOverWorkBranchFix
positions.frame: Should the engine's consult and navigate reads resolve against the run's own work branch, or against the base ref the run was cut from?
positions.why: src-8 reports Scarlet's ruling and src-9 is that ruling verbatim ('the base ref is correct'); src-10 states the resulting implementation — reads now target the base ref, not the run's own freshly-committed branch; src-11 draws the boundary of that fix, clarifying that the document under extraction still reaches the engine only through pass one, never through these reads; src-12 states the consequence — one warm graph shared by both reads, immune to the run's own commit. Read together as the decision-and-consequence arc that answers the diagnosis in the prior group.

positions.name: PromptCacheTTLDecoupling
positions.frame: How does inter-stage dead time interact with Anthropic's prompt-cache TTL, and what TTL keeps the cache win independent of that dead time?
positions.why: src-13 asserts the coupling between the dead time and the prompt cache; src-14 quantifies the exposure — the default five-minute ephemeral cache is already shorter than the measured 336-second gap, risking a silent, error-free cost reversion on a slightly slower document; src-15 records Scarlet's approval of a one-hour TTL specifically to decouple the cache win from the dead time. Read as one arc from exposure to approved remedy, distinct from the graph-cache fix above though triggered by the same underlying dead-time finding.
